entry.title: SAP Ariba is now integrated with Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP
entry.content:
Quick link to GitHub.

Supply chain is a critical topic in almost every industry these days. We live in times where a controversial social media post and actions of government officials can disrupt factory operations almost the next day. See this Reuters (2025) article that sheds light on car production halt in Germany caught in the crossfire of political turmoil in 2 other countries. SAP Ariba helps diversify the risk between buyers and suppliers in tightly interconnected supply chains.

What a juicy target for cyber criminals one might say 😉

Therefore, meet the new kid on the blog when it comes to Microsoft Sentinel for SAP integration – SAP Ariba.

This cloud-native integration adds real-time threat detection, investigation, and response to your SAP Ariba environment and puts it into the context of your wider IT estate.

The bigger picture

Attackers use the easiest way in. Each month the SAP Security Patch Day starts a new race between hackers and defenders despite responsible disclosure obligations to allow a head start to defenders on reported vulnerabilities etc.

 

This race wears down defenses eventually – a gap is deemed to happen. Therefore, you need to be prepared to identify attackers in your IT landscape and be quick to lock them out again before they reach valuable targets.

Seeing the context and trail of the interconnected signals that the attacker leaves behind are key to identify compromise.

 

How It Works

  1. Create an application on your Ariba Developer portal to allow access to the audit-search api and collect your API key.
  2. Deploy the latest Microsoft Sentinel integration package in SAP Integration Suite. See this video for a similar scenario for guidance. Ignore the ERP specific settings on Cloud Connector etc. They don’t apply to Ariba.

3. Configure a Destination on SAP BTP for your Ariba instance and the audit search api.

 

Property

Value

Description

Name

Ariba-[TenantId]

Destination name (e.g., Ariba-p2pTeSap-2)

Type

HTTP

Connection type

URL

https://[region.]openapi.ariba.com/api/audit-search/v2/[prod or sandbox]

SAP Ariba Audit Search API URL (Find your base URl under Configuration Details)

Proxy Type

Internet

Always internet because of the cloud nature of the SAP service

Authentication

OAuth2ClientCredentials

For productive use

Client ID

[ClientId]

if applicable

Client Secret

[ClientSecret]

if applicable

Token Service URL

[TokenEndpoint]/v2/oauth/token

Ariba OAuth token endpoint

 

Additional Properties

Value

Description

tenantId

[TenantId]

SAP Ariba tenant ID

apiKey

[apiKey]

Api key for your SAP Ariba tenant

4. Deploy the agentless data connector for the Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP. See this video for a walk-through of the first steps and the official Microsoft Learn page here. Ignore the ERP specific settings on Cloud Connector etc. They don’t apply to Ariba

5. Connect your Ariba flow on the data connector pane to start ingesting SAP Ariba logs.

6. On the Advanced section supply the path “/community/SAPAribaAuditSearch” to point at the default route of the Ariba iFlow on SAP Integration Suite.

 

Observe the message flowing on Cloud Integration and Microsoft Sentinel. You can use the following query to verify the Ariba logs. Filter by AgentGuid in case you have multiple connections:

SAPAuditLog
| where AgentGuid == "Ariba"

 

Congratulations, you have successfully onboarded SAP Ariba to Sentinel for SAP 😎

 

There is one more thing!

Many of you are fronting Ariba with the SAP Cloud Identity Services. When you consult the attack graph from the beginning of this post, you already know that this is an important signal in the attack story. Identity compromise remains the number one attack path even in 2026. Have a look at the Digital Defense Report 2025 for more details.

Onboard your SAP Cloud Identity Service amongst your SAP BTP subaccounts to Sentinel for SAP from here to close that loop.

 

What you see is what you get

  1. AI enabled unified Security Operations
  • Correlate SAP Ariba events with enterprise telemetry in Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP and Microsoft Defender XDR ready for Microsoft Security Copilot.
  • Use prebuilt analytics rules, workbooks, and SOAR playbooks to detect and respond to threats like:
    • Privilege escalations
    • Unauthorized configuration changes
    • Suspicious transactions
  1. Compliance-Ready Log Retention
  • Store SAP logs cost-efficient in Microsoft Sentinel Data Lake for up to 12 years.
  • Support threat hunting involving SAP on the Sentinel Data lake through KQL jobs.

What’s Next

  • Enriching the mapping of the Ariba logs further to activate the remaining analytic rules provided by the SAP ERP private cloud offering.
  • Adding further Ariba specific detections. Which ones are top of mind for you? Reach out to me.

Final Words

That's a wrap 🎬 you saw today how simple SAP Ariba integration with your SIEM product can be. Remember: bringing SAP apps under the protection of your central SIEM isn't just a checkbox - it's essential for comprehensive security and compliance across your entire IT estate.

Quick link to GitHub.

Feel free to reach out to talk more SAP Ariba.

Cheers, Martin

entry.title: SAP Project Systems -BAPIs (A quick overview)
entry.content:
Core Project Management BAPIs

  • BAPI_PROJECT_MAINTAIN: The primary BAPI for creating and maintaining complete project structures including project definitions, WBS elements, networks, and activities.
  • BAPI_PROJECTDEF_CREATE: Create individual project definitions.
  • BAPI_PROJECTDEF_GETDETAIL: Retrieve detailed information about project definitions.
  • BAPI_PROJECTDEF_GETLIST: Obtain a list of project definitions based on selection criteria
  • BAPI_PROJECTDEF_EXISTENCECHECK: Check whether a specific project definition exists.
  • BAPI_PROJECT_GETINFO: Read detailed information about project definitions and their WBS elements.

WBS Element BAPIs

  • BAPI_BUS2054_GETDATA: Retrieve detailed information about WBS elements.
  • BAPI_BUS2054_CREATE_MULTI: Create multiple WBS elements in batch operations.
  • BAPI_BUS2054_CHANGE_MULTI: Modify multiple WBS elements simultaneously.
  • BAPI_BUS2054_DELETE_MULTI: Delete multiple WBS elements.
  • BAPI_BUS2054_GET_STATUS: Read active system and user statuses for WBS elements.

Network Activity BAPIs

  • BAPI_BUS2002_GETDATA: Get detailed data for network headers.
  • BAPI_BUS2002_CREATE: Create network headers.
  • BAPI_BUS2002_CHANGE: Modify network header information.
  • BAPI_BUS2002_ACT_CREATE_MULTI: Create multiple network activities.
  • BAPI_BUS2002_ACT_CHANGE_MULTI: Change multiple network activities.
  • BAPI_BUS2002_ACT_GETDATA: Retrieve detailed data for network activities.
  • BAPI_BUS2002_ACTELEM_CREATE_M: Create activity elements for networks.

Budget and Cost BAPIs

  • BAPI_COSTACTPLN_POSTPRIMCOST: Post primary costs for overhead planning.
  • BAPI_COSTACTPLN_READPRIMCOST: Read primary cost data from cost planning
  • BAPI_COSTACTPLN_POSTACTINPUT: Post activity input for cost planning.
  • BAPI_COSTACTPLN_READACTINPUT: Read activity input data

Resource Planning BAPIs

  • BAPI_ACTIVITYTYPE_GETDETAIL: Get detailed information about activity types.
  • BAPI_ACTIVITYTYPE_GETLIST: Retrieve list of activity types using selection criteria.
  • BAPI_ACTIVITYTYPE_GETPRICES: Determine activity prices for cost center/activity type combinations.
  • BAPI_ACTTYPE_CREATEMULTIPLE: Create multiple activity types.
  • BAPI_ACTTYPE_CHANGEMULTIPLE: Change multiple activity types.

Status Management BAPIs

  • BAPI_BUS2001_SET_STATUS: Set or revoke system and user statuses for project definitions.
  • BAPI_BUS2002_SET_STATUS: Set or reset network statuses.
  • BAPI_BUS2054_GET_STATUS: Read active system and user statuses for PSP elements.

BAPI Usage Examples

BAPI Name

Use Case

Input Parameters

Output

BAPI_PROJECT_MAINTAIN

Create complete project structure

Project definition data, WBS hierarchy, methods table

Project created with all components

BAPI_BUS2054_GETDATA

Extract WBS element details

WBS element key, language

Detailed WBS element information

BAPI_PROJECTDEF_GETLIST

Get project list for reporting

Company code, selection criteria

List of matching project definitions

BAPI_BUS2002_ACT_CREATE_MULTI

Batch create network activities

Network number, activity data table

Created activities with confirmation

Integration Considerations

  • Dependencies: PS BAPIs integrate with Controlling (CO), Materials Management (MM), and Financial Accounting (FI) modules.
  • Authorization: Requires appropriate PS authorization objects and transaction codes (CJ01, CJ02, CJ20N, etc.)
  • Performance: Use "Multi" BAPIs for batch operations to improve performance when processing multiple objects.
  • Initialization: PS BAPIs require calling BAPI_PS_INITIALIZATION before execution and BAPI_PS_PRECOMMIT before final commit.
  • Commit Handling: Use BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT after successful BAPI_PS_PRECOMMIT, or BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK if errors occur

Related Function Modules

  • BAPI_PS_INITIALIZATION: Initialize PS BAPI processing environment.
  • BAPI_PS_PRECOMMIT: Validate all PS BAPI calls before final commit
  • Function modules in SE37: Search for "BAPI*" with business objects BUS2001 (Project Definition), BUS2054 (WBS Element), and BUS2002 (Network) for additional PS-related BAPIs.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud: Cross Company Code Purchasing O...
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, you can indeed use a cross‑company code purchasing organization for both Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfers (5HP) and Advanced Intercompany Sales (5D2) — there is no technical restriction in standard. These scope items are designed to support multi‑company scenarios.

Take into consideration the below points while going with the above approach

Master Data Consistency: Ensure vendor/customer assignments, intercompany pricing conditions, and tax codes are harmonized across all company codes.

Reporting: Since one purchasing org spans multiple companies, reporting at the purchasing org level will aggregate across them. Use company code/plant filters for granular reporting.

Authorizations: Carefully design roles so users don’t inadvertently gain access across companies.

Compliance: Intercompany tax and pricing must still be maintained per company code; the cross‑company purchasing org doesn’t override these requirements.

entry.title: Help With Transaction Notification
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have created two TN's on sales order which is working fine.

Working TN:


---- Prevent addition/ update of Sales Order if DocDueDate or ShipDate is Weekend or Holiday----

/* ROW LEVEL CHECK (ShipDate) */
IF EXISTS (
SELECT 1
FROM RDR1 T1
WHERE T1.DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del
AND (
DATENAME(WEEKDAY, T1.ShipDate) IN ('Saturday','Sunday')
OR CAST(T1.ShipDate AS DATE) IN (SELECT CAST(StrDate AS DATE) FROM HLD1)
)
)
BEGIN
SET @error = 1717
SET @error_message = 'Sales Order line Ship Date cannot fall on a Weekend or Holiday.'
GOTO TN_RESULT
END


/* HEADER LEVEL CHECK (DocDueDate) */
IF EXISTS (
SELECT 1
FROM ORDR T0
WHERE T0.DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del
AND (
DATENAME(WEEKDAY, T0.DocDueDate) IN ('Saturday','Sunday')
OR CAST(T0.DocDueDate AS DATE) IN (SELECT CAST(StrDate AS DATE) FROM HLD1)
)

)
BEGIN
SET @error = 1718
SET @error_message = 'Sales Order Due Date cannot fall on a Weekend or Holiday.'
GOTO TN_RESULT
END

This is working fine.

However, When I consolidate these into a single TN, it doesn't work.

Is this system behavior or something missing in the code:

----------------------------

Consolidated TN not working

--------------------------------

IF EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM ORDR T0
    INNER JOIN RDR1 T1 ON T0.DocEntry = T1.DocEntry
    WHERE T0.DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del
      AND (
            -- Weekend/Holiday check for SO Due Date
            DATENAME(weekday, T0.DocDueDate) IN ('Saturday', 'Sunday')
            OR T0.DocDueDate IN (SELECT H.StrDate FROM HLD1 H)

            -- Weekend/Holiday check for Row ShipDate
            OR DATENAME(weekday, T1.ShipDate) IN ('Saturday', 'Sunday')
            OR T1.ShipDate IN (SELECT H.StrDate FROM HLD1 H)
          )
      AND T0.DocDueDate >= CAST(GETDATE() AS date)
)
BEGIN
    DECLARE @daytype NVARCHAR(100)

    SELECT TOP 1
        @daytype = CASE
                       WHEN DATENAME(weekday, T0.DocDueDate) IN ('Saturday','Sunday')
                            THEN 'Weekend (Header DocDueDate)'
                       WHEN DATENAME(weekday, T1.ShipDate) IN ('Saturday','Sunday')
                            THEN 'Weekend (Row ShipDate)'
                       WHEN T0.DocDueDate IN (SELECT StrDate FROM HLD1)
                            THEN 'Holiday (Header DocDueDate)'
                       WHEN T1.ShipDate IN (SELECT StrDate FROM HLD1)
                            THEN 'Holiday (Row ShipDate)'
                   END
    FROM ORDR T0
    INNER JOIN RDR1 T1 ON T0.DocEntry = T1.DocEntry
    WHERE T0.DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del
      AND (
            DATENAME(weekday, T0.DocDueDate) IN ('Saturday', 'Sunday')
            OR DATENAME(weekday, T1.ShipDate) IN ('Saturday', 'Sunday')
            OR T0.DocDueDate IN (SELECT StrDate FROM HLD1)
            OR T1.ShipDate IN (SELECT StrDate FROM HLD1)
          )

    SET @error = 1717
    SET @error_message = 'Date cannot be on a ' + @daytype + '!'
    GOTO TN_RESULT
END

 

Can someone please check and let me know what is missing for the consolidated TN to make it work?

Question posted in Q&Z: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/help-with-transaction-notification/qaq-p/14324378

Regards,

Joseph

entry.title: Limitations with RFQ and Supplier Quotations
entry.content:
Hi Community, 

We are currently facing some limitations with the standard RFQ and Supplier Quotation process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, similar to what is described in SAP Note 3600086 - Limitation with Supplier Quotation.

Specifically:

  • Once an RFQ is set to Published, it cannot be edited.

  • Suppliers are not able to submit partial quotations or quotations with fewer or more items than the original RFQ.

This is quite limiting for our customer, as suppliers often respond with:

  • Alternative items as replacements for requested materials, or

  • Partial quotations when they are unable to deliver all requested items.

Given these constraints, we are looking for recommendations on how to best use the standard functionality as-is, while still enabling a more flexible and realistic sourcing process.

Has anyone encountered a similar requirement, or found workarounds, best practices, or process design suggestions within standard SAP?

Any input or shared experience would be highly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: In SAP preventive maintenance work order basic start...
entry.content:
What you’re seeing is actually standard SAP scheduling logic in Plant Maintenance (PM) — the system is not “ignoring” your 7‑day factory calendar, but rather applying the rules of basic dates vs. scheduling dates.

  • Basic Dates (start/finish) are entered manually or derived from the maintenance plan.
  • Final Due Date comes from the maintenance plan cycle (e.g., monthly, yearly).
  • When you schedule, SAP checks the factory calendar assigned to the work center/plant.
  • Even if you’ve defined a 7‑day calendar, the system still respects the “normal scheduling logic”:
    • It calculates the start date backwards from the due date using the work center scheduling parameters (e.g., 5 working days lead time).
    • If the due date falls on a non‑working day (Saturday in your case), SAP shifts the date to the nearest valid working day.

Why it looks like 5 Days instead of 7 is because, the work center capacity category and scheduling parameters may still be set to a 5‑day week, even though the factory calendar is 7 days. In PM, both the factory calendar and the work center capacity settings must align. If the work center is defined with Monday–Friday as working days, the system will schedule accordingly, regardless of the factory calendar. That’s why your order is showing 31 Jan (Saturday) as “non‑working” and pushing the basic start date earlier.

To fix this, perform the following

  • Check Work Center Capacity Settings (CR02/CR03) and verify the capacity category and operating time. Ensure the work center is set to use the 7‑day calendar.
  • Check Maintenance Plan Scheduling Parameters and confirm the cycle and offset are aligned with 7‑day scheduling. Reschedule the Order after correcting the work center calendar assignment.

entry.title: Join Us Live: Ingesting SAP S/4HANA Master Data into SAP Customer Data Platform
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, efficiently managing and integrating customer data is key to improving operations and enhancing customer experiences. Our upcoming live session, "Ingesting SAP S/4HANA Master Data into SAP Customer Data Platform," is designed to help you navigate this critical aspect of data management.

Session Overview

Scheduled for March 19th, this 1.5-hour session will provide a practical demonstration of integrating SAP S/4HANA Master Data with SAP CX Customer Data Platform. Participants will gain a clear understanding of the steps involved in the full integration setup and its execution. The session aims to equip you with the knowledge needed to utilize customer data effectively in both B2B and B2C scenarios.

Key Objectives

  1. Understanding Integration Endpoints: Explore the integration endpoints of SAP S/4HANA and SAP Customer Data Platform. This foundational knowledge is crucial for establishing efficient connectivity between these platforms.

  2. Ingesting Sample Master Data: Learn how to ingest sample SAP S/4HANA Master Data into SAP CDP. The session will cover the technical aspects of data ingestion, offering practical insights.

  3. Generating Insights and Activating Data: Discover how to use ingested master data to generate actionable insights. We'll show you how to activate S/4HANA Master Data for effective B2B and B2C scenarios using Customer Data Profiles, Accounts, and Activities.

Who Should Attend?

This session is geared towards IT professionals, consultants, data engineers, and system integrators. If you're looking to improve your data strategy and understand the value of SAP integration, this session will provide valuable insights.

Join Us

Take this opportunity on August 27th to enhance your understanding of SAP systems and refine your approach to customer data management. This session is an ideal fit for those looking to deepen their knowledge and practical skills in SAP integration.

Register Here: Ingesting SAP S/4HANA Master Data into SAP Customer Data Platform

This live session offers a straightforward look at integrating and activating SAP S/4HANA Master Data. Join us to gain the insights needed to optimize your data-driven strategies. We hope to see you there.

entry.title: SAP University Alliances Excellence Award - Application Form >> Latin America & Caribbean 2026 <<
entry.content:
SAP Excellence Membership - Elevating Academic Partnership Through Recognition

The SAP Excellence Membership honors outstanding lighthouse institutions in the SAP University Alliances network. Recognition is awarded in three categories: 

1. SAP University Alliances Educational Excellence Membership (for outstanding curriculum contribution according to the SAP UA Portfolio Process, for outstanding achievements in SAP UA related competitions, for innovative ways to include SAP technology into academic teaching, innovative ways to utilize recent offerings like SAP Learning Hub, student edition, …) 
2. SAP University Alliances Research Excellence Membership (for outstanding research results and corresponding publications utilizing SAP technology / in the context of the SAP UA program, …)
3. SAP University Alliances Co-Innovation Excellence Membership (for outstanding engagement with the SAP ecosystem, e.g. via student projects on SAP Customer challenges, innovative POCs with SAP customers, SAP University Alliances
Lab activities, …) 

As a representative (lecturer) at an SAP University Alliances Member Institution, you can apply on behalf of your educational institution for one or more excellence membership awards by filling in this template and sending it to the global SAP University Alliances Inbox (universityalliances@sap.com) by March 31, 2026. 

👉For more details check out 📎 this guideline and in case of questions, do let us know!  

Best regards, 

Your SAP University Alliances team 

Latin America & the Caribbean

entry.title: Re: configuration missing and change showing only created...
entry.content: The error is because item category determination for LDPI is missing. In Public Cloud, you cannot add it yourself in CBC — it’s locked. You need to check scope item activation and, if still missing, raise an SAP incident so SAP can deliver the correct expert configuration.

entry.title: Paying with stablecoins in B2B scenarios - business process end-to-end
entry.content:
As an SAP S/4HANA user, you can pay your suppliers with stablecoins. Or receive payments in stablecoins. Key to it is SAP Digital Currency Hub. Here's a process-focused perspective on it.

Paying Supplier Invoices

Assuming your company is using SAP Digital Currency Hub, it's as easy to pay with stablecoins as paying the way you are used to: as an accounts payable accountant, you do a payment run. A dedicated payment method (you could use D for digital) would find all supplier invoices for business partners accepting stablecoins.

Payment Run in S/4HANA

Once the payment is released, it is sent via SAP's Multi-Bank Connector to the SAP Digital Currency Hub (DCH), which initiates payment immediately. It interacts with your custody provider, to process the stablecoin payment, and confirms execution of the payment, allowing to clear the payable in your accounts.

SAP Digital Currency Hub - Outgoing Payment

SAP Digital Currency Hub - Blockchain Transaction

Additionally, when doing the payment run, you should send a remittance advice from your ERP system, using the established ways like email or direct integrations, to the other party, detailing the payment's purpose.

Receiving Payment for Receivables

Assuming, you have communicated a dedicated destination address to your customer, they will direct their payment to it. SAP Digital Currency Hub communicates permanently with your custody provider to check for incoming payments on the blockchain networks. Once a payment is received, the address used tells, who has paid. Together with the payment date and amount, the customer's ID is reported back to your S/4HANA as an incoming payment.

As the currently used stablecoin format definitions (ERC-20) do not support fields like "note to payee" or "payment reference", a remittance advice, received on an established channel (see above), will help to reconcile this payment with an open receivable.

Destination addresses are a great way to receive payments. They can be seen as an alias for your wallet's address, generated only for a certain business partner. Hence, all payments received on this destination address can be attributed to them - hence only they know it.

Payment preparation in SAP Digital Currency Hub

As an accountant, you usually do not enter the SAP Digital Currency Hub, which is intended to run as a rather invisible component. Nevertheless, to establish the payment agreement for a business partner, you need to log in.

The payment agreement consists of a set of destination addresses.

  1. Receive-to addresses: For your customer, you will create a dedicated address they have to pay to. Payments received on this address will be attributed to this business partner. So, you need to communicate it to them.
  2. Pay-to addresses: As a customer, you will enter the destination address received from your supplier. You will direct all payments for this business partner to this address.

This allows to identify the payer based on the address he as paid to.

Optional: Replicating Receivables to SAP Digital Currency Hub

Receivables processing can be enhanced by replicating receivables to the hub. When an incoming payment drops in, it will reconcile the open receivable with these transactions. It applies a simple mechanism, determining the business partner by receive-to address (see above), and matching the receivable by amount, based on a FIFO principle. The benefit is enhanced note-to-payee information when the transaction is sent to the ERP solution, allowing instant clearing in accounts receivable.

Summary

Stablecoin transactions are fast, taking only seconds or minutes. They can be used to pay your suppliers, without modification of your ERP system. You only need to set-up a dedicated house bank account and a payment method, and to integrate with SAP Digital Currency Hub. Then, existing means like payment runs are used to trigger a payment, and bank statements received from the hub are used to receive payments. The hub itself does not need your attention, only at setup.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Performance Rating in Compensation
entry.content:
The configuration in the compensation form of the rating source have the following options other than default:  overallCompetency, overallObjective, OverallPerformance and overallPotential.

The default is the overallPerformance and that will give you the final rating as is configured in PM.   You also have the option to use the calculated rating and that should be set to No to ensure you get the manual rating vs. what is calculated.

Without seeing the actual configuration of the PM form and the Comp form it is impossible to give a definitive solution.   I would run a report against the PM form so you can see the values of all the ratings that are being produced in PM and that should help you with the mapping.

This should work but if it does not, you can store the rating outside of PM and map the pmRating to either an import key if not EC integrated or to an MDF if EC integrated.

entry.title: Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 Release – What’s New
entry.content:
With the 2602 release available, I’d like to share an overview on some of the innovations we have delivered in Warehouse Management. Details on every innovation delivered are also available within this What’s New presentation.

 

Core Function

Further Process Enablement with Transportation Management

As Transportation and Warehouse Management are naturally used jointly by many customers, we have enhanced the process coverage here. The following scope items can now be run by utilizing the various advantages of Transportation as well as Warehouse Management:

  • Customer Consignment (1IU)
  • Subcontracting Purchase Order (BMY)

Automatic Packing for Direct Goods Receipt Posting after Production Order Confirmation

This innovation completes the picture to have a very lean process to receive products from production. It is now possible to configure the process as follows:

  1. Goods receipt from production is triggered automatically upon production order confirmation
  2. The goods receipt is posted with a direct goods movement posting (no inbound delivery required)
  3. The received products are packed automatically according to packing instructions
  4. The putaway warehouse task is created automatically

The third step – the automatic packing – had not been supported in this process flow so far and is an innovation with this release. I consider this a great round-off to this process, especially as packing instructions can be maintained very reliably for products received from production. Before this innovation, you either had to pack manually or work with an inbound delivery which made the process more complex than necessary.

External Output Management for Handling Units

A use case very commonly required by many projects is the integration of parcel carriers, to register parcels and print the respective label provided by the carrier. There is no standard solution for this available in Warehouse Management, but there are multiple partner solutions on the SAP Store or projects build those on their own. The desired approach would be that the user can work with the app Pack Outbound Deliveries and print the carrier label from there. But what had been missing so far, was a trigger to request the label printing. With this new channel External Output Management, it is now possible not only to print a label directly from SAP, but to send the data to be printed via an XML file to an external output management system. The best part about this is, that customers can use the full flexibility of output management to determine when to trigger this message. Consequently, you could for example set up output management to send this message to an external parcel carrier system when the user hits the Print button in the app to thereby register the parcel and trigger the print-out of the carrier label.

Upgrade Storage Location to Warehouse Management in CBC

There are some customer projects that do not use Warehouse Management from the very beginning but start with Inventory Management only, often to reduce the project scope and complexity for the first go-live. In a second wave, it is then often desired to gain more efficiency by also leveraging Warehouse Management. Unfortunately, there is currently no easy way in CBC to convert the storage location accordingly. With this release, the CBC app Set Up Organizational Structure now allows the user to assign a warehouse number also to a regular storage location like Standard Storage Location for Finished Goods. With this, customers can continue using the same storage location but manage it with Warehouse Management. Of course, connecting the storage location to the warehouse number is only the first step, and further set-up steps are required. More details are described in this how-to guide and in this video.

User Experience

Enhancements to Fiori App: Manage Outbound Delivery Orders

The Fiori app Manage Outbound Delivery Orders is the successor for the app Run Outbound Process. Users had been notified of this deprecation process with the Experience Switch, providing them the option to provide feedback if they are missing any important features in the new app. We did receive such feedback, and we have acted on it and therefore implemented the following features:

  • Some item fields are now also displayed on header level, because they contain important information which the user wants to see on first glance. Examples for this are the staging area or shipping conditions.
  • There is a new button Create Pick Task Manually which simplifies the manual creation of picking warehouse tasks.
  • An improved serial number dialog has been introduced which simplifies the entry of serial numbers, especially if multiple serial numbers are to be scanned.

This is also a big thank you to the users who have provided their feedback, enabling us to recognize what is missing in the new app and to make improvements accordingly.

Joule for Warehouse Management

Naturally a highlight of this release is also the enablement for Joule. Users can now use Joule to interact with the most important business objects in Warehouse Management using natural language. This includes reading data but also creating or updating data as well as performing actions on business objects. The following business objects are supported so far: Outbound delivery order, inbound delivery, warehouse order & warehouse task, storage bin, physical stock and available stock. This will open new interaction patterns for users, helping especially less experienced users to complete their tasks.

 

Extensibility

CDS View for Production Material Request

As many customers are using the Advanced Production Integration (scope item 6NI), it is essential to expose the Production Material Request (PMR) data as CDS views. Many projects require this to retrieve the relevant PMR data for integrating with a Manufacturing Execution System (MES). One use case is to map the manufacturing order number to the PMR number, as the user in the MES will usually work with the manufacturing order number, but when working with single-order-staging, the reference in the physical stock is always the PMR number. To ensure a correct consumption posting, the manufacturing order number must be mapped to the PMR number in this case. Moreover, the assignment of bins to production supply areas with the respective settings on staging behaviour are also made available via CDS view. Concretely, these CDS views have been released:

  • I_EWM_ProdnMatlReqHeader
  • I_EWM_ProdnMatlReqItem
  • I_EWM_BinAssignmentToPSArea

 

Integration

Warehouse Order and Task – Send to External System

The new SOAP API Warehouse Order and Task – Send to External System is certainly one of this release’s highlights. It completes the picture for many projects that are looking to integrate warehouse automation systems such as conventional AS/RS, shuttles or robotics. Initially, such integration had to be done purely via the Warehouse Order and Task API: Projects had to implement a polling mechanism to check for newly created warehouse tasks that would be relevant for the warehouse automation. We already managed to improve this in 2502 when we introduced the Warehouse Order Events, allowing project to leverage those to be informed about newly created warehouse tasks right away, replacing the polling mechanism.

With this new API, the integration is simplified even more: In the SSCUI Define Queues, the Operating Environment of a queue can be set to 7 External System; No Resource Management.

With this setting, when a warehouse task is created for this queue, a SOAP message containing the comprehensive warehouse order & warehouse task data is automatically pushed down to a sub-system for which the destination can be specified via communication arrangement. In addition, those warehouse tasks are automatically locked for editing, thereby preventing accidental confirmation or cancellation by a user, as for those warehouse tasks, they are in the responsibility of the sub-system. Once processed by the sub-system, the confirmation can be send back using the Warehouse Order and Task API.

Physical Inventory API: Create Physical Inventory Document

The API Warehouse Physical Inventory has been enhanced with a create operation. While in the past, projects had mainly used the API for mobile applications to do a physical inventory count, we have also received requests from projects to enable the creation of physical inventory documents. That is to support an ad-hoc physical inventory for a mobile application, but also to integrate with warehouse automation systems that also cover physical inventory processes and need to replicate those physical inventory documents and results back to SAP. This new API provides the full flexibility for those processes and allows to create storage bin-specific as well as product-specific physical inventory documents.

 

FIND OUT MORE!

This blog only pointed out some of the highlights of this release – you can find the full list of innovations in the What’s New presentation.

entry.title: Re: C_S4CFi- Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Clou...
entry.content:
Hello @ZakariaELMartaoui @GabrielaTrasakova 

Thank you both for your question.

It is important to note that there is an ongoing transition in the way SAP certifications are written. The main changes are the shift from question-only format to performance based, scenario driven assessments. So, what you might end up doing will depend on the time of your exam.

These links will guide you:

https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/certification-support/certification-program

https://learning.sap.com/get-certified/reimagining-certification

https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/release-notes-learning-activities/q4-2025

For C_S4CFI and what to expect see the link below for the learning journey details.

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-financial-accounting-in-sap-s4hana-cloud

  • For the exam structure you should expect traditional question, mixed or fully practical scenario tasks.
  • The difficulty is usually close to the learning Journey scope.
  • You should prepare for configuration, process execution, troubleshooting, root cause thinking and integration.
  • The number of questions asked will be determined by SAP. Also, because changes in format and assessment style is ongoing. You can check the current exam entry in your Certification Hub before you schedule.
  • The exam will require your hands-on knowledge and familiarity.

I recommend you complete the full C_S4CFI learning journey in sequence, practice each major FI process in your system, build your checklist and practice.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Invoice Creation BAPI from Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hi @javier_alonso63 

Purchasing invoice is a document that you get from your vendor. The document provides the details of the purchase like PO number, product number, quantity, value, tax etc. That is system agnostic, it is just how purchasing invoice process works.

All you need to do is to pass that information to the BAPI and post the invoice in the system. Personally, I find nothing awkward in that.

In contrast MIRO pulls default values out of a purchase history to facilitate data entry. So the values can be just verified instead of typing manually.

You can not post a purchasing invoice based on a purchase history only. The invoice data has to come from a vendor.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 : Table Control Creation
entry.content:
Hello,

Table controls are usually classic SAP GUI ALV or Table Control, that you maintain through PERSONAS. Example VL03N 

 

If you have to create one, you must go on SAP GUI, create a Z tcode, a custom program behind with the handling of an ALV.

Then in your SMEN FLAVOR, you create button that calls your custom transaction with the ALV and here you have your table control that you can custom using PERSONAS.

Even better : if you have OPENUI5 librairies available, you can build a APPLET to include a fiori like component to display whatever you want to display.

Good luck to you!

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to read approvers via script on Sap CPQ?
entry.content:
Hi, 

we created a custom action in CPQ 2.0 as post action after approval. 

We added the following code: 

from Scripting.Quote import MessageLevel
from datetime import datetime

history = context.Quote.GetApprovalHistory()

approvers = []  # Approver name from ApprovalHistory
enrichedApprovers = []  # enriched Userdata ready for consumption
# === NEU: Finde den letzten "Submitted for Approval"-Zeitpunkt ===
submitted_timestamps = [
    entry.SubmissionDate for entry in history if entry.Status == "Submitted for Approval"
]
latest_submitted = max(submitted_timestamps) if submitted_timestamps else None

for entry in history:
    if entry.Status == 'Approved' and latest_submitted and entry.ResolvingDate > latest_submitted:
        approvers.append(entry.Approver)

unique_approvers = list(set(approvers))

I hope this helps!

BR, 
Tobias

entry.title: Re: Delivery address in Purchase order
entry.content:
Why Case 1 and Case 2 behave differently because

In Case 1, when you edit the delivery address immediately after creating the PO, the system has not yet “refreshed” the item buffer. Each item is still treated as having its own address reference, so the system doesn’t prompt you to update all items.

In Case 2, by switching tabs and coming back, you force ME21N to refresh the item buffer. At that point, SAP recognizes that multiple items share the same address number. When you change the address, the system correctly asks whether you want to update all items.


Workaround: After creating the PO, switch tabs (or save and re‑open) before editing the delivery address if you want the popup to appear.

Permanent Fix: This is standard behavior in ME21N; if it’s disruptive, you can raise an SAP incident. SAP may provide a note or correction if they consider it a bug in your release.

Alternative: If you want consistent address updates across items, consider using the header delivery address instead of item‑level addresses, so changes automatically apply to all items.

When creating a new task list, assign this profile to the task list and some fields like the calculation key are filled by default for each operation.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit – Migrate Data Directly from SAP System – Using Staging Tables
entry.content:
Hi all,  

Over the past few months, our customers have asked us whether there is any way to get more transparency on the selected data for the migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System. Customers have also asked whether there are any opportunities to adjust the selected data based on their requirements. 

The selection process for migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System is very complex. This made it difficult to adjust or even view the selected data in the Migrate Your Data app. 

However, with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition CE2602 and upcoming Private Cloud Edition release, we are happy to announce our new feature Staging Tables as Intermediate Storage for Direct Transfer.  

Most of you are familiar with the selection process of migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System. For SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, an RFC connection is used to connect to the source system. For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, a communication arrangement for communication scenario SAP_COM_0816 is used.  

Figure 1: Selection Process without Staging Tables  

Why use staging tables as intermediate storage? 

With this new feature, the Migrate Your Data app can create staging tables for the migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System in a remote SAP HANA schema and can store the selected data there. You can then adjust the values directly in the relevant database tables before continuing with the migration process. This option provides increased transparency for the selected data, as well as providing the opportunity to adjust data directly in the relevant database tables if required. 

Figure 2: Selection Process with Staging Tables  

You activate this feature when creating a migration project. During step 3, Staging Tables (Optional), you activate the feature and specify the relevant connection to the remote SAP HANA schema in the Database Connection field. 

You then need to activate staging tables for the relevant migration objects. 

Note that using staging tables is completely optional! 

When using staging tables as intermediate storage, the selection process is divided into two steps. In the first step, the relevant data is extracted from the ECC system to the staging tables. This step is triggered in the Migrate Your Data app using the action Extract to Staging Tables. 

In the second step the data is transferred from the staging table to the Migrate Your Data app. Again, this step is triggered in the Migrate Your Data app, this time using the action Transfer from Staging Tables. 

Once you have extracted data to the staging tables, you can not only view the data as a whole in the remote SAP HANA schema, but you can also adjust the data according to your requirements. That gives you more flexibility in preparing the data for the migration.   

Figure 3: Contents of staging table  

Note that the staging tables for the migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System cannot be used with the staging tables for the approach Migrate Data Using Staging Tables. The staging tables for the migration approach Migrate Data Using Staging tables are based on the template files for a migration object provided by SAP. The staging tables for the migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System are based on the data model of the migration objects.  

Let us know how you might use the staging tables for the migration approach Migrate Data Directly from SAP System and feel free to comment below!   

All the best for your project!  

Philipp

entry.title: Implementing Authorization Checks for Data Modification Using CDS Access Controls
entry.content:
Introduction

Using CDS Access control we can restrict data selection access. It allows you to put checks on the data using standard authorization object and limiting values for the same

e.g. This is one of the standard access control for Profit Center

In this blog, we will look at how access control can be used to control UI‑level modifications—for example, managing the visibility of Create, Edit, and Delete options—without the need for additional custom coding.

Backend Implementation

To begin, create a CDS view that lists all business objects requiring this authorization control. Add an entry for each operation—such as create or edit. For demo, I’ve included activity '02' (change).

Next, define an Access Control object that links your CDS view to the relevant authorization object. This authorization object will be maintained for the associated Fiori application. Set the required activity type based on the configuration in your CDS access control.
In this example, I’ve used the standard CDS view I_PROFITCENTER, but you can replace it with your own entity that needs authorization‑based restrictions.

 

When a user with the correct authorization views the CDS data,

User not having authorization for activity will see content as below

 

Once the CDS view is ready, include it as an association within your main entity that is exposed via your OData service:

...

association[0..1] to ZTEST_BO_AUTH as _BOAuth

on cdsviewname = 'I_PROFITCENTER'

{

...,

_BOAuth

}

Applying Authorization in the Fiori UI

 

In your Fiori application, bind the visibility or enabled property of Create/Edit/Delete buttons to the isEditable property provided by this child association:

{/ZTEST_BO_AUTH('I_ProfitCenter')/isEditable}

Get this entityset loaded at the beginning.

                        var oModel =  this.getView().getModel();

                     oModel.read("/ZTEST_CBO_AUTH", {

                    urlParameters: {

                        "$filter":"BusinessObjectID eq 'I_PROFITCENTER'",

                        "$orderby":"isEditable desc"

                    },

                    success: function (oData) {

                        if (oData?.results ) {

                              var bEditable = oData.results[0].isEditable; // boolean conversion

                        }

When a user without activity '02' opens the app, the Edit button will no longer appear.

Conclusion

Using CDS Access Controls is one of the clean way to enforce consistent authorization across backend services and Fiori UIs. Instead of scattering checks across controllers and handlers, developers can define access rules once and rely on CDS to enforce them everywhere. This low‑code technique reduces maintenance effort and makes the implementation much easier.

entry.title: Test, Demo, and Development: Discovery Package "How‑to" Hub for SAP Cloud ERP, Private Edition
entry.content:
This blog provides a curated collection of how‑to guides, practical tips, and step‑by‑step instructions based on real requests we have received from users working with the Discovery Package for SAP Cloud ERP, Private Edition. Its purpose is to help others facing similar questions complete their tasks more efficiently. In addition, several entries highlight known limitations or issues encountered in the process—offering transparency and guidance on how to navigate them. The hub continues to grow as new questions and requests come in.

Topics covered

  • Client Copy 

  • Change ADT to logon to a different client

  • Http error 429

 

Client Copy

It is possible to run a local client copy in the Discovery package system using TA SCCLN. TA SCC_CLIENT_SIZE can be used to check on the size of a client. Due to HANA compression, this is only an approximation if more than one client is filled. The tool also supports selective size determination for specified tables. As an example client 100 size info looks like this:  

Some useful tips: 

  • Client copies must no longer be executed from the target client. Instead, we recommend to run the Client Copy tool from a third client which is not affected by the tool. For example, when copying from client 100 to 200, you could run the tool in client 000. 

  • The client copy always locks the target client. By default, the source client is also locked. You may choose to not lock the source client at the risk of concurrent write access to the source client resulting in inconsistent copies. A client lock now not only prevents new logins using SAP GUI, but also for HTTP access. 

Here is an example of creating a new Client 101 and copying Client 100 into it. Using profile SAP_ALL copies almost all information. This is the pic after the copy.  

 

Change ADT to logon to a different client

If you want to logon ADT to a different client than the default client 100 (e.g. after a client copy) you can configure it as follows: 

In the https logon method, ADT uses the client specified in the profile parameter login/system_client -- client 100 in our system. This can be changed to a different client in TA RZ10 and requires a restart (see sevice requests in SAP for Me). Afterwards, ADT/Fiori/WebGUI will use this one as default logon client.  In Fiori and WebGui you can still specify other clients on the logon screen or by adding ?sap-client=xxx to the URL. 

 

Http Error 429

When an HTTP 429 error occurs, it means the HTTP client has sent too many requests within a short period of time. 

The TDD Discovery Package system is configured with a fixed capacity of 32 CPUs and 256 GB RAM. 
If an HTTP 429 error appears, you need to reduce the number of parallel requests, for example by lowering the number of simultaneous logons. 

Please note that the TDD system is not designed for load or performance testing.

entry.title: Re: PATCH operation failed on API_SALES_ORDER_SRV
entry.content:
Hello, @DEB1989 

The API_SALES_ORDER_SRV is external service doesn't support deep structures, which prevents them from being updated simultaneously with a single PATCH request.
One possible option is to create a batch request where described two requests for two different entities (A_SalesOrder, A_SalesOrderItem). Please refer to the examples provided in the screenshots and code listing.

1. Batch request should has POST HTTP method, URL example #1 URL

2. Set required Headers parameters. Please note the part of value which underlined in red: boundary = {name_batch}
#2 Headers

 3. Code listing of body (json). Provide the same name_batch like in previous step, and declare new boundary like on the second line boundary={name_boundary}

#3 Body

If you have any questions or problems, please let me know.

Best regards,
Aliaksandr.

entry.title: Re: staff scheduling on user level
entry.content:
Dear @Jana_Hauck,

In Public Cloud you generally can’t do HR-style staff rostering plan individual employee shifts + match/validate skills per person. Public Cloud is the digital core for execution (finance/logistics/service) while shift scheduling + skills-based rostering is delivered by dedicated workforce solutions

SAP options that do cover shift rostering + skills:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Scheduling is positioned exactly for demand-driven, skills-based, compliant shift scheduling
  • SAP Time and Attendance Management by WorkForce Software explicitly includes scheduling plus time/attendance/absence compliance
  • SAP Workforce Forecasting and Scheduling by WorkForce Software is also a dedicated scheduling product forecast + optimized schedules

Maintain skills for individual user this depends heavily on which kind of skills you mean:

Option A. Project staffing / professional services

Public Cloud does support employee qualifications for project staffing/resource management skills-like attributes used in project resource planning. That’s not shift rostering, it’s project/resource planning

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/configuring-managing-resources_af6b6c75-45ef-4b76-8722-8f7be580394c

Option B. Service technicians dispatching

If your shifts are actually field service assignments, SAP Field Service Management matches service call/activity requirements with technician skills on the planning/dispatch board

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_plan_dispatch/plan-dispatch-overview.html

When Public Cloud needs workforce master data, SAP supports replicating the Workforce Person to Public Edition using SAP Master Data Integration (MDI)

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/8d2cbcdb056e4461b313fbcb68160896.html

Summary:

  • Shift-by-shift employee rostering + skills-based scheduling NOT in Public Cloud core. I suggest you consider it use SuccessFactors Workforce Scheduling or WorkForce Software products
  • Skills/qualifications inside Public Cloud exist mainly for project staffing/resource management

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: 30 Years of SAP Partnership and Certified Excellence: cbs a Trusted Partner for SAP Transformation
entry.content:
With more than 30 years of SAP experience and as a multiple-time certified operations partner, cbs - Corporate Business Solutions Unternehmensberatung GmbH - supports industrial companies through complex SAP S/4HANA, cloud, and AI projects. In this interview, cbs explains how industry focus, quality standards, and end-to-end services ensure reliable digital transformation.

 

Can you briefly introduce your company and explain which services you offer?

cbs, Corporate Business Solutions, designs digital end-to-end business process solutions for international industrial clients. With over 3,000 global projects, more than 30 years of market presence, dedicated industry know-how, and a unique, comprehensive offering for business transformation, we support our customers in establishing the next generation of enterprise solutions. Our global consulting organization includes the logistics subsidiary Leogistics and the HR consultancy Projekt0708, our own near- and offshore centers, and a strong partner network. In addition to its headquarters in Heidelberg, cbs is present at more than 40 offices worldwide. More than 1,700 employees across Europe, Asia, and the Americas manage projects and SAP operations for customers worldwide. From SAP strategy to project management and change management to implementation and application management. Business consulting is also a rapidly growing area for us. Here, we deal with topics such as target operating models and inventory optimization.

 

You have been working with SAP for 30 years since its founding. How would you describe your experience so far, and are there any special events or milestones in this partnership that stand out in your memory?

Our founders were former SAP employees. As a result, we had excellent relationships with SAP from day one. This led to us being recommended to customers time and time again, and we lived up to these recommendations by delivering successful projects. It is also always important for us to understand which products or solutions SAP is developing so that we can make informed decisions about where to invest. We focused on GTS early on, trained employees, and carried out successful projects. We passed on the references to SAP so that we could do more business together.

The same applies to SAP S/4 HANA. We also focused on this topic early on, sending employees to Walldorf to participate in product testing, contribute our knowledge, and build new knowledge. We carried out initial projects at an early stage and then led the first SAP S/4HANA migration at Viessmann, a globally active manufacturing company. This was a showcase that DSAG presented at its 2019 annual congress together with the CIO of Viessmann and Christian Klein. We have a good relationship with the SAP Executive Board. We maintain close contact, and Christian Klein and Thomas Saueressig have attended our events on several occasions.  


In 2015, you decided for the first time to have your service portfolio certified by SAP - initially for SAP Business Suite solutions operations and, from 2021 onward, for SAP S/4HANA solutions operations. What motivated you to take this step at the time, and how do you evaluate this decision today?

It is in cbs's DNA to develop and build on its own topics. However, this means that we have little opportunity or knowledge of how our competitors are performing in these areas. On the other hand, we lacked a “seal of quality” to give our customers a certain degree of security. We were able to achieve both with the certification. The audits enabled us to identify areas for improvement, and passing the certification gave us proof we can use, especially when acquiring new customers. We have now completed the certification for the fifth time and still see the added value that certification brings us in the areas mentioned above. 

 

 

From your perspective, what has changed for the better since participating in the SAP Certification Program for Operations Partners?

Whereas knowledge of SAP products/modules used to be the main focus, the program has now shifted to emphasize the IT service process. I think this is absolutely essential and appropriate for certification as an SAP Operations Partner. At the same time, the program is adapted each time to reflect current relevant topics, such as cloud, RISE, Clean Core, or AI.


What makes your company stand out in particular, and how do you support different industries in their digital transformation?

Our industry focus is on the manufacturing sector. This includes traditional manufacturing companies, machine and plant manufacturers, electronics manufacturers, automotive suppliers, as well as customers in the process industry, pharmaceutical industry, and medical technology. We are convinced that it adds value for the customer when we understand the processes, are familiar with the problems typical of the industry, and have already solved them.

In this industry, many customers are still on their way to SAP S/4 HANA. In the context of this topic, however, a complete transformation project is often initiated so that additional SAP solutions are also introduced. Current examples include SAP EWM, Business Data Cloud, and SAP SuccessFactors.

We offer our customers all of this from a single source. To successfully implement large projects, our program and project managers now have comprehensive PMO support. These services handle test and cut-over management, for example. We have established our own change management division because we have seen that change management consultants in the market often lack SAP knowledge and understanding of SAP projects. We also offer consulting for the entire SAP Toolchain.

We are of the “Selective Data Transition Engagement”. Our cbs ET Enterprise Transformer helps to migrate data from ERP Systems (or other sources) to the new S/4 HANA System in a selective approach. Our application management services are also used in S/4 projects. Since projects often run for several years, but customers want to use their own employees in the project, we take over the management of the existing SAP landscape or provide support in the new landscape once the customer is live.

Apart from SAP S/4 projects, we have a large division in the field of BTP and AI. Here, we digitize processes that were not previously mapped in SAP at all, but rather across multiple systems, and in some cases also involved intermediate steps on paper. We bring these together in a SAP BTP solution, thereby accelerating the process, improving quality, and increasing transparency. We are increasingly using AI to further simplify and accelerate processes. With our AI-based text recognition, we achieve recognition rates of more than 98% and can create the corresponding SAP documents, e.g., sales order, purchase order, or invoice, directly from a piece of paper.

 
SAP is consistently aligning its strategy toward the cloud and encouraging partners to actively support their customers in migration. How are you currently supporting your customers in their cloud transformation, and do you plan to place an even stronger focus on cloud services in the future?

Functionally, we cover all SAP solutions that our customers need, whether on-premise or in the cloud. We are one of the few providers that can offer customers all services if they want to implement a full cloud solution (SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP CX, SmartRecruiters, SAP SuccessFactors, Concur, Ariba, IBP, DM, SAP BTP, etc.). Our strategy is to deliver cloud projects whenever possible. For example, if we are short on resources and have to decide between a cloud project for one customer and an on-prem project for another, we would go with the cloud project. As the functionality is rising with every release, we recommend that every customer check if a cloud solution fits the requirements. From our point of view, the cloud solution is the go-to solution for every customer.

 

What significance do the certifications have for your marketing activities and for communication with your customers?

The certification provides us with external proof of our performance, our knowledge, and our service processes. We are always happy to use this in our acquisition processes.

 

 

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners and an overview of all certified partners can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com or on the SAP Partner Portal.

entry.title: Fiori Migration to SAP BTP: Best Practice for Multi-Backend (Region-Based) Routing Without SAP Gatew
entry.content:
Hello community,

I am working on a Fiori migration project and would like guidance on the recommended architecture for handling multiple backend systems by region after moving to SAP BTP.

Current  Architecture

  • An on-premise SAP Gateway hosts a set of Fiori apps.

  • The Gateway forwards OData calls to multiple backend systems (ECC) based on system alias.

  • This setup allows region-based routing, but currently all backends are technically reachable, and multiple backends may be called during app execution.

Target Architecture

  • Decommission the on-premise SAP Gateway system.

  • Host Fiori apps on SAP BTP and expose them via SAP Build Work Zone.

  • Keep the same backend ECC systems (one per region).

  • No central Gateway system remains for system-alias-based routing.

Our Proposed Approach

  • Define one SAP BTP Destination per backend system (e.g. one per region).

  • Use the same destination names across subaccounts (DEV / QAS / PRD), with destination details varying per subaccount.

  • Configure the Fiori app to call all destinations and aggregate the results on the UI side.

     

Questions

  1. Is this approach (calling all backends and aggregating results in the UI) considered a good practice on SAP BTP?

  2. Are there recommended alternatives for region-based backend routing without a central SAP Gateway?

  3. How is user-region–based backend selection typically handled in BTP-centric Fiori architectures?

Any reference architectures or SAP-recommended patterns would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: Managing Performance Forms for Employees on Global As...
entry.content:
Hi @DP4 

With the available business rule scenario Trigger onPostSave Events for Job Information, you can launch a Performance Management form. One possible option would be using as a condition Event Reason = Add Global Assignment so when that happens a new form is created. 

The steps on how to configure it are documented here: 

2530838 - Initiate PM Form On Job Change Event: How to Launch a New Performance Form Based on a Job Info Change https://me.sap.com/notes/2530838/E

As for the existing performance goals,  the Manage Individual Talent Data Transfer tool, where you can transfer data from an inactive employment (source) into a target employment would not be applicable since the Home assignment is paused (dormant) which does not qualify as inactive employment. 

However, the option Transfer Goal Between Users would allow to transfer the performance goals between the userID (home) and userID-1 (host).

Hope this will help, 

Regards, 

Jordi

entry.title: Withholding Tax is not being calculated when posting invoices
entry.content:
During the posting of incoming invoices. When Section Code is maintained in the Basic Data tab, the system fails to calculate withholding tax. However, when the Section Code is removed, the withholding tax is calculated correctly.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Withholding Tax is not being calculated when posting...
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

Please find the details below:

  • If the section code is not passed during invoice posting, the system performs TDS calculation directly on the invoice amount, which is the standard and expected system behavior.

  • If the section code is passed at the time of invoice posting, the system evaluates the supplier-level exemption threshold and triggers TDS calculation only after the cumulative transaction amount exceeds the configured exemption limit.

Kindly refer the KBA 3703336 - Inconsistent 194Q Withholding Tax calculation when posting supplier invoices via Create Incoming Invoices app for more information.

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud
